data.title: Coinbase to buy Solana’s trading platform Vector.fun
data.description:
Coinbase acquired the Solana-based marketplace Vector. fun. The Tensor Foundation, which launched and governed the DEX, will remain independent and focus on the Tensor NFT marketplace.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/coinbase-buy-sol-trading-platform-vector-fun/

data.title: Michael Saylor responds to MSCI exclusion risk: Index classification cannot define strategy
data.description: Michael Saylor, founder and executive chairman of Strategy, responded to questions about the risks of being excluded from the MSCI index in a post on the X platform. He stated that Strategy, as a publicly traded and operating company, is fundamentally different from funds, trusts, and holding companies. Strategy not only has a $500 million software business but also uniquely uses Bitcoin as productive capital in its fund management. Index classification cannot define Strategy. The company has a clear long-term strategy, a firm belief in Bitcoin, and its mission remains to become the world's first digital currency institution based on sound money and financial innovation.

data.title: Federal Reserve officials are speaking out in quick succession: hawks and doves are at loggerheads, and the probability of a December rate cut is soaring.
data.description: Several senior Federal Reserve officials delivered speeches, providing the market with the latest insights into the outlook for monetary policy, inflation trends, and the impact of emerging technologies on the financial system. Their remarks revealed significant internal divisions within the Fed on the core issue of when to cut interest rates. The Fed officials' speeches triggered market volatility and adjustments in expectations. Analyst Adam Button stated that US stocks received substantial buying support after Fed official Williams indicated that "given the current policy being somewhat restrictive, a rate cut is still possible in the near future." Williams' dovish signals reignited the debate over interest rate policy. His comments pushed the probability of a Fed rate cut in December to approximately 60%, putting pressure on the dollar. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Governor Milan: If he becomes the key voter, he will support a 25 basis point rate cut.
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Mirren said on Friday that yesterday's (US September non-farm payrolls) data had a "clearly dovish" impact. Mirren pointed out that the current lack of data does not mean we have no forecasts; we should be forecast-oriented, not data-oriented. Mirren stated, "If my vote is the deciding factor, I will support a 25 basis point rate cut." Mirren believes that labor market data was not as strong as expected, and CPI data is not expected to be released until after the next FOMC meeting. (Jinshi)

data.title: US stocks opened with mixed results for the crypto sector, with Coinbase rising 1.31%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, the Dow Jones Industrial Average opened 165 points higher, the S&P 500 rose 0.38%, and the Nasdaq Composite gained 0.47%. The crypto sector was mixed, with Coinbase up 1.31%, BitMine up 0.32%, Circle down 0.66%, and Strategy up 0.05%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, including US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: UBS: Next week's data is expected to increase the probability of a Fed rate cut before the end of the year.
data.description: UBS Global Wealth Management strategists stated in a report that the US dollar may weaken due to weak US data expected next week. Key data includes Tuesday's retail sales, consumer confidence, and pending home sales, while Wednesday will see durable goods orders, weekly initial jobless claims, and new home sales. The strategists noted that this data will influence market expectations for economic growth, inflation, and Federal Reserve policy. "We still expect the upcoming data to be weak enough to push market expectations upwards towards a December rate cut, which will put pressure on the dollar before the end of the year." (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve's Williams indicated a possible rate cut "nearly," prompting traders to increase their bets on a December rate cut.
data.description: Traders increased their bets on a third consecutive rate cut by the Federal Reserve at its December meeting on Friday, after Fed official Williams indicated the Fed might cut rates "in the near future." Short-term interest rate futures now reflect a 57% probability of a December rate cut, significantly higher than the previous reading of below 50%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Grayscale transferred 1808 BTC and 6568 ETH to Coinbase.
data.description: According to Arkham, Grayscale just transferred 6,568 ETH (worth $17.97 million) and a total of 1,808 BTC (worth $151.63 million) to a Coinbase Prime address.

data.title: Binance Launches Baby Shark Universe Trading Competition with Token Rewards
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is set to introduce the Baby Shark Universe Trading Competition on Binance Alpha. This event invites users to trade Baby Shark Universe (BSU) tokens through their Binance Wallet (Keyless) or via Binance Alpha, offering exclusive token rewards during the promotion period. The competition is open to all users eligible to trade Binance Alpha tokens, running from 2025-11-21 16:00 (UTC) to 2025-12-05 16:00 (UTC).

Participants will be ranked based on their total purchase volume of BSU tokens during the promotion period. The top 4,900 users will share a pool of 2,597,000 BSU tokens, with each receiving 530 BSU. Only trades executed through Binance Wallet (Keyless) or Binance Alpha qualify, excluding third-party dApp transactions. The competition focuses solely on cumulative purchases, with selling activities not considered. There is no cap on trading volume for participants, although transactions related to token bridging are ineligible.

To participate, users must update their Binance App to the latest version, create and back up a Binance Wallet (Keyless), and trade BSU during the promotion period. Rankings will be automatically calculated post-event, and winners can claim rewards on the event page. Binance emphasizes adherence to the Activity Terms, which include compliance with Binance's general terms and conditions, privacy policy, and prize promotion terms. Only users from qualified regions who complete account verification (KYC) and maintain an active Binance Wallet are eligible. Binance reserves the right to disqualify participants engaging in dishonest behavior or tampering with program code. The platform may amend the Activity Terms at its discretion, including changes to eligibility, winner selection, and event duration.

data.title: UK consumer confidence slumps ahead of difficult budget
data.description:
Public confidence in the UK has declined across all metrics as households prepare for potentially harsh budget announcements.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/uk-consumer-confidence-slumps/

data.title: Michael Saylor posted "Hold on," responding again to the market downturn.
data.description: Strategy founder and executive chairman Michael Saylor responded to the market downturn again on the X platform, writing "Endure." Previously, Michael Saylor had also posted that "they will say we were lucky," implying that the current market low may be a buying opportunity.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Logan: The Fed needs to "keep interest rates unchanged for now"
data.description: Dallas Fed President Logan said on Friday that the Federal Reserve needs to keep interest rates “unchanged for now” while assessing the current level of monetary policy tightening. In a speech in Zurich, she reiterated her previous view that the Fed’s October rate cut was not justified given that inflation remains high and the labor market is broadly balanced. She stated, “Keeping interest rates unchanged for a period of time, without clear evidence to support further policy easing, will allow the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) to better assess the constraints of current policy.” (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitmine's Ethereum holdings have surpassed BlackRock's.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, after Tom Lee's Bitmine purchased 63,123 ETH this week (worth $174 million), its ETH holdings have reached 3,623,002 ETH (worth $9.96 billion), surpassing BlackRock to become the largest holder of Ethereum. Meanwhile, BlackRock reduced its holdings by 43,237 ETH today (worth $119 million), currently holding 3,604,966 ETH (worth $9.91 billion).

data.title: Federal Reserve's Logan: Expects the Fed's balance sheet to resume growth soon.
data.description: Federal Reserve official Logan stated that he expects the Fed's balance sheet to resume growth soon, and the Fed will need to offset the tailwinds from financial conditions. Maintaining interest rates will allow the Fed to assess the tightening situation. (Jinshi)

data.title: Fed's Logan: Another rate cut unlikely in December
data.description: Federal Reserve official Logan indicated that another rate cut in December is unlikely. (Golden Ten)

data.title: Federal Reserve's Balance Sheet Expected to Grow Soon, Says Logan
data.description: According to Odaily, Federal Reserve official Logan has indicated that the Federal Reserve's balance sheet is anticipated to resume growth in the near future. Logan emphasized the need for the Federal Reserve to counteract the tailwinds brought by financial conditions. Maintaining the current interest rates will allow the Federal Reserve to assess the tightening conditions effectively.

data.title: GSR integrates market making, over-the-counter trading, and fund management into GSR One.
data.description: Cryptocurrency liquidity provider GSR announced the expansion of its institutional trading and fund management platform, GSR One, unifying market making, systematic OTC trading, and fund management into a single system. This upgrade enables clients to gain real-time insights into all aspects of market activity, including order book depth, customized analytics, programmatic execution tracking, enhanced market-making insights, and direct wallet control and security management. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Federal Reserve Vice Chairman: It may be too early to say whether AI will influence the Fed's monetary policy decisions.
data.description: Federal Reserve Vice Chairman Thomas Jefferson stated that he believes there is a clear distinction between the rise of artificial intelligence and the dot-com boom of the late 1990s. In prepared remarks for an event at the Cleveland Fed on Friday, Jefferson said that many internet companies of the late 1990s and 2000s "achieved little profit, only speculative revenue prospects, even though they received external funding and were relatively easy to list on exchanges amidst investor enthusiasm." Jefferson added that valuations of AI-related companies, measured by price-to-earnings ratios, are still far below the peak levels of internet companies. He said that at the height of the dot-com bubble, there were over 1,000 publicly traded companies ostensibly focused on internet companies, while today there are only about 50 publicly traded companies focused on artificial intelligence. Jefferson also stated that it may be too early to judge the impact of AI on the Fed's monetary policy decisions. He added that the financial system remains robust and resilient.

data.title: Arthur Hayes: Don't rush to sell, the bottom is coming soon.
data.description: Arthur Hayes posted on the X platform that the insufficient liquidity of BTC has led to an excessive drop, and people should not rush to sell. The bottom is coming soon. He said that the US stock market should also crash, and further money printing is expected, which requires a crash in artificial intelligence technology stocks.

data.title: Crypto Firms Urge U.S. Government for Swift Policy Action
data.description: According to Odaily, over 60 cryptocurrency companies and advocacy groups have submitted a 'pre-Christmas list' to U.S. President Donald Trump this week. The letter outlines more than a dozen measures they hope the government will prioritize during the congressional review of related legislation. Led by the Solana Policy Institute, the letter urges the Treasury Department and the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) to promptly implement most of the policy initiatives from the July Presidential Digital Asset Working Group report. Signatories, including Uniswap, Ledger, FalconX, and Paradigm, emphasize that these actions could yield 'quick wins' and support Trump's goal of making the United States the 'world's crypto capital.'
